title: Comments & Clean Code 📝
description: Write notes in your code that only humans can read.

slides.title: What Are Comments? 💬
slides.content:
# Talking to Humans, Not Computers

**Comments** are lines of text in your code that JavaScript **completely ignores**. They exist only for **human readers**.

Imagine you're writing a recipe 🍳. The instructions are for the cook (the computer), but you might also add personal notes like "Mom's secret ingredient" — that note is a comment!

Comments help you:

* 📝 **Explain** what your code does
* 🧠 **Remember** why you wrote something
* 👥 **Communicate** with other developers
* 🐛 **Temporarily disable** code during debugging

slides.title: Single-Line Comments 📏
slides.content:
## The // Syntax

Use **two forward slashes** (`//`) to create a single-line comment. Everything after `//` on that line is ignored:

```javascript
// This is a comment — JavaScript ignores this!
console.log("Hello"); // This part is also a comment
```

You can place comments:

* **Above** a line of code → to explain what the next line does
* **At the end** of a line → to add a quick note
* **On their own** → to separate sections of code

**Key Rule**: The `//` only affects the **rest of that line** — not the next line!

slides.title: Single-Line Comments Example 💻
slides.content: Notice how comments are completely ignored in the output:

quizData.question: Which symbol starts a single-line comment in JavaScript?
quizData.explanation: JavaScript uses // for single-line comments. # is used in Python, and <!-- is used in HTML.

slides.title: Multi-Line Comments 📄
slides.content:
## The /* */ Syntax

Need to write a longer comment that spans multiple lines? Use `/*` to start and `*/` to end:

```javascript
/* 
  This is a multi-line comment.
  It can span as many lines as you need.
  JavaScript ignores everything between /* and */
*/
```

Multi-line comments are perfect for:

* 📖 **Explaining complex logic** that needs a paragraph
* 🚫 **Disabling large blocks** of code at once
* 📋 **Adding file headers** with author info and descriptions

⚠️ **Warning**: Multi-line comments **cannot be nested**! You can't put `/* */` inside another `/* */`.

slides.title: Multi-Line Comments Example 💻
slides.content: Multi-line comments are great for longer explanations:

quizData.question: How do you write a multi-line comment in JavaScript?
quizData.explanation: Multi-line comments in JavaScript start with /* and end with */. Everything between them is ignored.

slides.title: Commenting Out Code 🚫
slides.content:
## A Powerful Debugging Trick

**Commenting out** means adding `//` or `/* */` to **temporarily disable** code without deleting it. This is one of the most common debugging techniques!

```javascript
console.log("Step 1");
// console.log("Step 2"); // Temporarily disabled!
console.log("Step 3");
```

Why is this useful?

* 🔍 **Isolate problems** — disable code to find which line causes a bug
* 🧪 **Test alternatives** — keep old code while trying new approaches
* 💾 **Preserve work** — don't lose code you might need later

Once you've fixed the issue, simply **remove the comment markers** to re-enable the code!

slides.title: Clean Code Best Practices 🌟

quizData.question: What does 'commenting out' code mean?

options.text: Temporarily disabling code using comment syntax

quizData.explanation: Commenting out means using // or /* */ to temporarily disable code without deleting it — very useful for debugging!
